steph-thrive-notes:
💡 DANCER STRENGTH & PERFORMANCE PROGRAM
Source: @frankiebiondi.physio | Saved Mar 4, 2026

A 6-week small group strength program for competitive dancers, led by a registered physiotherapist (MScPT).

DETAILS:
• In-person, 8–10 dancers per group
• Thursdays starting July 2, 2026
• Location: Happiness is Dancing, Sudbury ON
• $600/dancer (tax included) + 8-week home program
• Age groups: 7 & under / 8–9 / 10–12 / 13+
• Limited spots — first come first served

WHY IT WORKS:
• Physio-led = trust + premium positioning
• Niche-specific (dancers only)
• Multi-week commitment = recurring revenue
• Age-tiered = wide audience from one program

THRIVE APPLICATION:
Could do something similar — e.g. Pilates for Dancers, Postpartum Strength, or Teen Athlete Performance. Partner with a local physio or RMT. Small group (6–10), multi-week, seasonal intensive. Great way to fill the barn outside regular class schedule.

steph-thrive-notes-v2.title: 🏛️ Flohaus Pilates — Studio Inspo
steph-thrive-notes-v2.body:
Source: @flohauspilates | Saved Mar 13, 2026

Beautiful studio interior — chandelier, floor-to-ceiling windows, warm neutral palette. White sofa, velvet armchair, tiled coffee table, Persian rug. Very elevated, lived-in feel.

Notes: love the chandelier as a centrepiece, the mix of textures (leather, velvet, tile), and the natural light.

steph-thrive-notes-v2.title: 🏋️ Sunny Space Studio — Studio Inspo
steph-thrive-notes-v2.body:
Source: @sunnyspace.studio | Saved Mar 13, 2026

Reformer Pilates studio with beautiful arched mirror wall detail, backlit arches, warm timber floors, grey/concrete walls. Pilates Allegro reformers, dumbbell rack. Great natural light from large windows.

Notes: love the arched mirrors with LED backlight — creates depth and makes the space feel larger. The mix of concrete texture and warm wood floors is a great combo.

steph-custom-recipes.name: Chocolate Choco Bar Ice Cream
steph-custom-recipes.desc: @yuvrajdewan · Homemade Magnum-style chocolate ice cream bars · Makes 5
steph-custom-recipes.body:
Makes 5 choco bar ice creams
You'll need: 5 slim long Ziploc bags

CHOCOLATE SHELL:
• 200g dark chocolate

CREAMY CHOCOLATE FILLING:
• 500g whipping cream
• 40g cocoa powder, sifted
• 1 tsp vanilla extract
• 200g condensed milk

INSTRUCTIONS:

Step 1 — Chocolate Shell:
1. Melt dark chocolate until smooth and glossy.
2. Pour evenly into 5 Ziploc bags and coat the inner sides by gently pressing and spreading.
3. Set aside slightly.

Step 2 — Filling:
1. Beat whipping cream until slightly thickened (NOT stiff peaks — keep it soft and fluid).
2. Fold in sifted cocoa powder, vanilla, and condensed milk.
3. Beat until completely smooth and combined. Strain if lumpy.
4. The mixture should be thin and pourable. If too thick, microwave in 5-second intervals until it loosens.
5. Pour into chocolate-lined bags. Seal properly.
6. Freeze UPRIGHT (not flat) for at least 4 hours.

To serve: Cut open bag, slide out, crack through the shell into the creamy center.

steph-custom-recipes.name: Rosemary Olive Shortbread
steph-custom-recipes.desc: Nosh & Nibble p.49 · Savory slice-and-bake · Makes ~24
steph-custom-recipes.body:
Makes ~24 | Prep 30 min + 30 min chilling | Cook 35 min

Ingredients:
1 cup kalamata olives, finely chopped
1/4 cup olive oil
4 garlic cloves, finely chopped
2 rosemary sprigs, finely chopped
2 cups flour
2 tsp garlic powder
1.5 tsp kosher salt
8 tbsp unsalted butter, room temp
5 oz sharp aged cheese (Gruyere, Parmesan, or sharp cheddar), grated
1 egg yolk
1 tbsp ice water

Instructions:
1. Fry olives in oil ~15 min until deep purple-brown. Stir in garlic and rosemary off heat.
2. Whisk flour, garlic powder, salt. Add olive mixture. Work in butter with hands. Mix in cheese, egg yolk, ice water until dough holds.
3. Shape into 8-inch log, freeze 30 min.
4. Preheat 350F. Slice into 24 pieces just under 1/2 inch thick.
5. Bake 28-35 min until crisp and golden. Cool completely.

Note: Swap olives for sun-dried tomatoes for a sweet variation.

steph-custom-recipes.name: No-Churn Salted Honey Lemon Ice Cream
steph-custom-recipes.body:
Serves 6-8 | Prep 10 min + 8 hrs freezing

KEY TIP: Whip heavy cream to STIFF PEAKS first. Reduce lemon juice to 3/4 cup (not full cup) to prevent icy texture.

Ingredients:
1/2 cup granulated sugar
3 tbsp grated lemon zest (about 6 lemons)
3/4 cup fresh lemon juice
1/2 cup honey, plus more for serving
2 tsp kosher salt
2 cups heavy cream
Flaky salt and lemon twists for serving

Instructions:
1. Whip cold heavy cream in a dry bowl to stiff peaks.
2. Rub sugar + lemon zest together until pale yellow and fragrant.
3. Add lemon juice, honey, salt - stir until sugar dissolves.
4. Gently fold lemon mixture into whipped cream.
5. Freeze at least 8 hours.
6. Serve with honey drizzle, flaky salt, lemon twist.

steph-custom-recipes.name: Applesauce Hemp Heart Muffins
steph-custom-recipes.desc: No added sugar · toddler + mom approved · Makes 12 · Uses oat flour + almond milk
steph-custom-recipes.body:
No added sugar · Toddler + mom approved · Makes 12

WET:
• 1 cup unsweetened applesauce
• 2 eggs
• ¼ cup coconut oil (melted) or butter
• ¼ cup almond milk (or any milk)
• 1 tsp vanilla

DRY:
• 1 cup oat flour (or rolled oats blended)
• ½ cup whole wheat flour
• 3–4 tbsp hemp hearts
• 1½ tsp baking powder
• ½ tsp baking soda
• 1 tsp cinnamon
• Pinch of salt

MIX-INS (pick one or both):
• ½ cup blueberries
• ⅓ cup chocolate chips

INSTRUCTIONS:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Grease or line a muffin tin.
2. Whisk all wet ingredients in a large bowl.
3. Add all dry ingredients and fold gently — don't overmix.
4. Fold in blueberries or chocolate chips.
5. Fill each cup ¾ full.
6. Bake 18–20 min until a toothpick comes out clean.
7. Cool 5 min before removing.

Keep: Fridge 5 days · Freezer 3 months 🧊

steph-kids-stories-rush.title: Stinky Steve and the Missing Sock
steph-kids-stories-rush.body:
Once upon a time, in the back of a sock drawer, there lived a very stinky sock named Steve.

Steve wasn't like the other socks. While they smelled like lavender and fresh laundry, Steve smelled like... well, Steve.

"Has anyone seen my match?" Steve called out one laundry day.

All the other socks backed away slowly.

"Maybe check the gym bag," squeaked a little ankle sock.

So Steve went to the gym bag. And there — tangled up with a sweaty headband and three granola bar wrappers — was his missing match.

"STEVE JR!" he cried.

Steve Jr. was even stinkier than Steve.

Together, they were unstoppable.

The end. 🧦

steph-kids-stories-rush.title: Stinky Steve and the Sock Showdown
steph-kids-stories-rush.body:
It was Saturday morning, and all the socks were arguing.

"I'm the comfiest!" bragged the fuzzy purple one.
"I'm the fastest!" shouted the neon running sock.
"I'm the fanciest!" said the sparkly gold sock, flipping her cuff dramatically.

Then Stinky Steve yawned from the corner. "I'm the stinkiest," he said proudly.

Everyone groaned.

"That's not something to brag about, Steve!"

But Steve grinned. "Oh yeah? Let's have a contest! The Great Sock Showdown!"

So they did.

Round one was the Stretch Test. Steve gave it his all… and accidentally slingshotted himself straight into a pile of clean laundry. "Ten points for style!" yelled a baby sock.

Round two was the Slide Race across the hardwood floor. They zoomed off — but Steve's stink left such a strong trail that the others started slipping and spinning out. Steve crossed the finish line sideways, yelling, "Victory stinks!"

The final round was the Sniff Test. The cat sniffed the first sock. Purr. The second sock. Purr. Then it got to Steve… and ran away so fast its tail puffed up like a balloon!

All the socks burst out laughing. Even the fancy gold one fell over giggling.

"Okay, okay," she said. "You win, Steve. You're officially the funniest and smelliest sock in the drawer."

Steve took a bow. "I'd like to thank the gym shoes, the laundry basket, and of course, my nose."

The end. 🧦🏆😂

steph-kids-stories-rush.title: Cub the Grizzly
steph-kids-stories-rush.body:
Deep in the forest, under tall pine trees and big snowy mountains, lived a little grizzly bear named Cub.

Cub was strong. Cub was fast. And Cub loved to play rough. He liked to tumble, roll, and pounce in the grass.

"RAWR!" Cub would say as he jumped onto his friend Moose. Moose wobbled and laughed. "Cub! You're a rough player!"

Cub didn't mean to be rough. He was just excited.

One day Cub saw Bunny hopping through the meadow. "Let's play!" said Cub.

Cub pounced. Boop! Bunny rolled over in the soft grass.

"Cub!" Bunny giggled. "You're a little too strong!"

Cub's ears drooped. "I just want to play," he said quietly.

Old Mama Bear watched from under a big tree. She walked over and gave Cub a warm bear hug.

"Cub," she said gently, "you have a big grizzly body and a big grizzly heart. You just need to remember to use gentle paws with small friends."

Cub thought about that.

The next day he saw Bunny again. "Want to play?" asked Cub.

"Okay!" said Bunny.

Cub tiptoed closer. Instead of pouncing, he rolled softly beside Bunny. They tumbled in the grass. They laughed. They chased butterflies.

And when Moose came by, Cub gave him a big rough bear wrestle. Moose loved it. Because Moose was big enough.

That night Cub curled up in his cozy den. Mama Bear tucked him in.

"You played well today," she whispered.

Cub yawned a big grizzly yawn. "I'm a rough bear," he said sleepily.

"Yes," Mama Bear smiled. "But you're also a kind bear."

Cub closed his eyes. The forest grew quiet. The moon glowed over the mountains. And little Cub the grizzly dreamed of tumbling, playing, and using his gentle paws.

Goodnight, Cub. Goodnight, forest. 🌙🐻

steph-kids-stories-rush.title: Cub and the Big Forest Game
steph-kids-stories-rush.body:
In a wide green forest, under tall trees and bright stars, lived a little grizzly bear named Cub.

Cub loved to run, climb, and wrestle. He was very strong for a little bear. Sometimes when Cub played, he would tumble so hard that whoosh! everyone would roll over. But Cub was a very nice bear.

One morning Cub woke up early. The sun peeked through the trees. "Today is a perfect day to play!" Cub said.

He ran to the meadow where his friends were. Bunny was hopping. Squirrel was climbing. Moose was munching grass.

"Let's play the Big Forest Game!" said Cub.

"What's that?" asked Squirrel.

"It's a game where we run, hide, and chase!" said Cub excitedly.

"Okay!" said everyone.

"Ready… set… GO!"

Cub ran fast. Too fast! He zoomed past Bunny and made the grass whoosh. Bunny tumbled over.

"Oh!" said Cub, stopping quickly. "Sorry Bunny. I forgot my gentle paws."

Bunny stood up and shook the grass off. "That's okay," she said. "You're just a big, strong bear."

Cub thought for a moment. Then he had an idea.

"I'll be the tree guard," Cub said. "I'll stay by the big tree and tag anyone who runs by!"

The friends loved that idea. They ran around the meadow. Squirrel zipped past the tree. Tag! Cub gently tapped him. Moose trotted by. Cub gave him a playful nudge. And Bunny hopped right between Cub's paws.

Everyone laughed and laughed.

When the sun started to go down, the friends lay in the grass together.

"That was the best game," said Bunny.

"Yeah," said Squirrel.

Moose nodded. "Cub is the best tree guard."

Cub smiled a big grizzly smile. He liked playing rough. But he liked being a good friend even more.

That night Cub snuggled into his cozy den. Mama Bear tucked him in.

"Did you have a good day?" she asked.

Cub yawned. "The best," he said softly.

Outside, the forest grew quiet. The stars twinkled. And little Cub the grizzly fell asleep dreaming of running, playing, and all his forest friends.

Goodnight, Cub. 🌙🐻

steph-notes.title: ✝️ Hayes Baptism Checklist
steph-notes.body:
ITEMS TO BRING:
• Cross and chain
• Olive oil
• Bar of soap
• One large white candle (lambatha)
• 2 smaller white candles
• One white bath sheet
• One white sheet
• Two white hand towels
• Undergarments
• Outfit & shoes

GODPARENT REQUIREMENTS:
• Must be 12+ years old
• Must be a practicing Orthodox Christian in good standing
• Must present Baptism certificate to church at booking
• If married — must present Ecclesiastical wedding certificate
• Marriage must have taken place in an Orthodox Church

FEES (St. Nicholas Greek Orthodox, Sudbury):
• Each parent membership (2 yrs): $200/each
• Each godparent membership (2 yrs): $200/each
• Church fee: $275
• Priest & Chanter: $150
• Deposit: half due at booking (non-refundable)
• Remainder: due at least 1 month before

NOTES:
• Baptismal name: Ζαχαρίας (Zachary)
• NOT available: Holy Week, Holy Pascha, last weekend of July, Aug 1–15

steph-kids-activities.name: Spray Bottle Painting
steph-kids-activities.desc: Hang a white sheet on a clothesline outside and let kids spray watered-down paint from spray bottles to create colourful abstract art. Open-ended, sensory, and gloriously messy! Focus is on the process not the result.
materials: Two trees or posts to hang the line
